name: Roads Valle de Mena — circular from Artziniega
name_original: Carreteras Valle de Mena — circular desde Artziniega
region_intro: Road cycling around Artziniega offers diverse terrain within the natural environment of the Cuadrilla de Ayala in Álava. The region features a varied landscape of mountains, valleys, and forests, providing options for different cycling abilities. Notable features include the Sierra Salvada mountain range with its limestone walls and high plateaus, and the green, hilly Ayala Valley. Paved roads lead to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains, such as those found in Sierra Salvada. Best road cycling routes around Artziniega The most popular road cycling route is Roads Valle de Mena — circular from Artziniega, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route features an elevation gain of 889 meters. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de Ro – Old Bridge of Balmaseda loop from Artziniega, a moderate 36.4 miles (58.6 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and views, including an elevation gain of 662 meters. Local road cyclists also love the Carretera A-3634 – Llanteno Schools loop from Artziniega, a 27.2 miles (43.9 km) trail leading through the hilly landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Artziniega is defined by the dramatic Sierra Salvada mountain range, the verdant Ayala Valley, and a network of paved roads. The region offers routes for various ability levels, from easier loops to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains. The routes in Artziniega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Artziniega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Artziniega?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Artziniega, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Artziniega?
A: The terrain around Artziniega is quite varied, ranging from the green, hilly landscapes of the Ayala Valley to the more challenging ascents of the Sierra Salvada mountain range. You can expect paved roads with significant elevation gains, including climbs like those found in Sierra Salvada, and more relaxed stretches through valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the Artziniega area?
A: Yes, the region offers challenging climbs, particularly within the Sierra Salvada mountain range. Passes like Peña Angulo and Orduña are frequently part of routes with significant elevation gains. Some sections feature ramps up to 20%, providing a demanding experience for seasoned cyclists.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while road cycling around Artziniega?
A: Road cycling routes offer stunning views of the Sierra Salvada's impressive limestone walls and high plateaus. The descent from the Orduña pass provides spectacular panoramic vistas. You might also encounter natural highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1353866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maroño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5828668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Grande Summit&lt;/a&gt;, depending on your chosen route.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Artziniega?
A: Yes, many routes around Artziniega are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033317786/roads-valle-de-mena-circular-from-artziniega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roads Valle de Mena — circular from Artziniega&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24576120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera A-3634 – Llanteno Schools loop from Artziniega&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the town.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Artziniega?
A: While specific seasonal details are not provided, the region's diverse natural environment suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can also be enjoyable, though some climbs might be more strenuous in warmer weather.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options around Artziniega?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are easier options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31301823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gordexola – Oxirando Tower loop from Artziniega&lt;/a&gt; is rated as easy, making it a good choice for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. It's always recommended to check the route's elevation profile before setting out.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route in Artziniega?
A: Artziniega, being a town, generally offers parking facilities. While specific parking locations for cyclists are not detailed, you can typically find parking in or near the town center, which serves as a convenient starting point for many routes.

Q: Can I reach Artziniega and its cycling routes using public transport with my bike?
A: Information on public transport policies for bikes in Artziniega is not readily available. It's advisable to check with local public transport providers directly regarding their regulations for carrying bicycles before planning your trip.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Artziniega?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the dramatic natural beauty of the Sierra Salvada, and the network of paved roads that allow for diverse and engaging rides.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Artziniega itself has charming villages and historical sites, suggesting that you will find cafes and establishments for refreshments within the town and potentially in other villages along the routes. Planning your stops in advance, especially on longer rides, is always a good idea.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on a typical moderate route around Artziniega?
A: Moderate routes around Artziniega typically feature significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31912031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Ro – Old Bridge of Balmaseda loop from Artziniega&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate path, includes an elevation gain of 662 meters over its 58.6 km distance.
tips: Road in good condition, with horizontal markings. Virtually no shadows and little traffic.

The road that goes near the town of Partearroyo, you can see the views of the Ordunte Reservoir and its extension along the foot of the Montes de Ordunte. In the area there is a series of pretty villages typical of the area.

Magnificent ascent to enjoy the landscape and the calm where a lot of minor cycling races in the province are circulated. It has a very regular percentage throughout the rise and only in half of it are percentages that reach 9%. There are views of the Cadagua valley through the Mena pass.
Small reservoir located in the Vall de Mena and close to the town that bears the same name as the reservoir, Arceniega.
